pi_items.source: Task description line 4 and setup_task.sh line 20 match the metadata value
setup_task.sh line 37 creates this file at exactly this path
Task description explicitly mentions 'at tooth positions #3 and #14' and verifier uses these position numbers
Universal Tooth Numbering System (used in US dentistry): tooth #3 = Upper Right First Molar (not Second Molar). FDI system would denote this as #16
Universal Tooth Numbering System (used in US dentistry): tooth #14 = Upper Left First Molar (not Second Molar). FDI system would denote this as #26
Task description states: 'Standard placement (if height >= 10mm) or Sinus lift required (if height < 10mm)'. Verifier code line 141 and 160 use 10.0 as the threshold
Verifier code line 129 retrieves tolerance_mm from metadata with default value 3.0. Line 134 and 153 use this tolerance for measurement accuracy comparison
setup_task.sh line 32 sets 'pos_3_height_mm = 8.5' and verifier line 127 defaults to this value
setup_task.sh line 33 sets 'pos_14_height_mm = 12.2' and verifier line 128 defaults to this value
setup_task.sh line 34 sets pos_3_decision to 'Sinus lift required' (correct since 8.5mm < 10mm threshold)
setup_task.sh line 35 sets pos_14_decision to 'Standard placement' (correct since 12.2mm >= 10mm threshold)

privileged_info_summary: The task evaluates subantral bone height measurements at two posterior maxillary tooth positions using Blue Sky Plan dental software. Ground truth values are: Position #3 has 8.5mm bone height requiring sinus lift, Position #14 has 12.2mm bone height suitable for standard implant placement. The 10mm threshold separates clinical decisions. Measurements within 3mm tolerance of ground truth are considered accurate. However, the task description incorrectly identifies tooth #3 as 'Upper Right 2nd Molar' when it is the Upper Right 1st Molar in the Universal Tooth Numbering System, and similarly misidentifies tooth #14 as 'Upper Left 2nd Molar' when it is the Upper Left 1st Molar. The data is synthetically generated mock values, not real DICOM data.
